task_completion.description: Log in to the ManageEngine ADAudit Plus console.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine ADAudit Plus web interface is visible, showing the user is logged in (e.g., dashboard or settings page).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the PowerShell auditing configuration section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the configuration page for PowerShell auditing or PowerShell execution events within ADAudit Plus.
The text 'corp.acmefinancial.com' is entered into the domain name input field.
The text 'DC01.corp.acmefinancial.com' is entered into the domain controller input field.

task_completion.description: Enable the Script Block Logging option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle for 'Script Block Logging' is checked or set to enabled.

A 'Save' button is clicked, or a dialog/message appears showing the configuration is being saved, or a validation error (like 'Access Denied' or connection failure) is shown as a result of attempting to save.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with the ADAudit Plus GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is interacting with the ADAudit Plus web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, API calls, or scripts.

integrity.description: The configuration is done within the actual ADAudit Plus application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are typed into the genuine ADAudit Plus web form fields, not just written in a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of browser developer tools (Inspect Element) being open or used to alter the page content.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target domain: 'corp.acmefinancial.com'. Target domain controller: 'DC01.corp.acmefinancial.com'. The 'Script Block Logging' option must be enabled.
